_comment: User aggregate lifecycle machine, restricted to the disable/enable status lifecycle (states derived from Modelith enum UserStatus; events from User actions disable/enable). PLACEMENT (ARCHITECTURE.md section 7): ephemeral in-process, load-act-save inside the single write Tx the Command Layer owns; the status persists as the graph node `status` attribute in LadybugDB. CONCURRENCY SERIALIZATION: read-modify-write within one write Tx; cross-process writes serialize on LadybugDB's single-writer lock (ErrLocked handled by CommandExecution/DBLocked). On load the runtime rehydrates to context.status; `initial` is the create-time status (Active). SCOPE NOTE: register/changePassword/assignRole/login/logout are owned elsewhere (crm.session and repo create paths); this machine covers only the Active<->Disabled status transitions the disable/enable actions drive. Disabling a User has a downstream effect on Sessions (session-active-user), enforced by the Session machine, not here.

_delays.persistRetryBackoff: 500 ms - ErrLocked backoff; retriesExhausted at 3 attempts (~1.5s total)

rolledBack._exhaustive: both domain states (Active, Disabled) can enter the persist overlay, so priorStatus ranges over {Active, Disabled}; both priorIs* guards are present
